titleEn: Recommendation for Enhancement of Cash Retention Limit and revision of limits to branches
contentJson:
{"details":"

On review of Cash Holding position of branches in our Region for the past 1 year, it was observed that

  • Region has exceeded allotted Cash Retention Limit (Rs 19.90 Crore) over 80% of days in FY25-26 (293/365 days)
  • Large excess observed after continuous holidays / long weekend (Sometimes in excess of Rs 12 Crores), major reason of excess being bulk remittance of collection by TASMAC outlets (aggregate of collections on holidays)
  • Over 20 Branches in our Region are acting as cash collection points for over 70 TASMAC outlets (average collection per outlet per day is over Rs 3 Lakh)
  • Region does not have a Currency Chest owing to which remittance of excess is being done at Madurai Currency Chest periodically (however the same is challenging owing to extent of spread of the Region, presence of branches in remote/hilly areas)
  • Region has 67 ATM/CDM (Previous allocation has not accounted for number of ATM/CDM attached to particular branches)
  • In FY 25-26, 1 new Branch (Kosavapatti) and 4 new CDMs (Balakrishnapuram, Vangamanathu, Andipatti, Kosavapatti) have been added, revision of Cash Retention Limit is required to account for these additions as well.
  • Majority of Jewel Loan borrowers prefer disbursement in Cash.
  • 2 more branches, 3 more CDMs have already been proposed to be setup by Q1 in FY 26-27

Department Recommendation:

Based on historical data and conservation estimate of Rs 3Lakh per TASMAC outlet and Rs 15 Lakh per ATM, close to Rs 13 Crore (per day) of the Region's limit is locked for these two activities (Majority of TASMAC collections are in small denomination notes, owing to which most of the collected notes can only be remitted to Currency Chest).

Moreover, average daily growth in Jewel Loan over the last 2 quarters has been around Rs 4 - 5 Crore per day - majority Jewel Loan disbursement is through cash.

Considering (1) historical Cash position in branches (after control of cash movement from Regional Office), (2) cash requirement for JL disbursement, (3) proposed increase in branches, CDMs in current Fiscal, we may request Banking Operations Department, CO to consider allotting Cash Retention Limit of Rs 27 Crores for our Region for FY 26-27.

Recommended Branch wise allotment is attached for reference.

titleEn: Request for sponsoring IOB branded tickets to Sri Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple
contentJson:
{"deptName": "PLAN", "details": "

Introduction

Our Palani Branch (0376) has identified a strategic opportunity to enhance the visibility and brand recall of our Bank through sponsorship of IOB-branded thermal tickets used at Arulmigu Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple.

Observations

Palani Branch presently maintains a substantial share of the Temple’s deposit portfolio. The Branch has observed exceptionally high devotee footfall at the Temple throughout the year. Even during lean periods, the Temple attracts approximately 2–3 lakh devotees per month, while during festival and peak seasons, such volumes are witnessed on a daily basis. Devotees visiting the Temple originate from various parts of the country, thereby providing a unique and large-scale branding opportunity for the Bank. In this connection, the Branch proposes to sponsor IOB-branded thermal ticket rolls used for Temple entry/access. Such branding is expected to significantly improve the visibility of the Bank among the general public and reinforce our association with one of the most prominent pilgrimage centres in the country. The Branch has obtained a quotation from Mukil Printers, the regular supplier of thermal ticket rolls to the Temple authorities, for the following: 300 Nos. of 2-inch printed thermal rolls (15 metres) 500 Nos. of 3-inch printed thermal rolls (40 metres) The total cost for the above requirement is Rs.68,250/-. The proposed quantity is estimated to cater to approximately 5,00,000 tickets and is expected to last for nearly two months.

Recommendations

Considering: the immense daily public exposure, the nationwide reach among devotees, the existing deposit relationship of over Rs.30 Crore maintained by the Temple with our Bank, and the strong potential for further business development, we may recommend sponsorship of the IOB-branded thermal tickets at a total cost of Rs.68,250/- to Public Relations Department, Central Office.", "signatorySnapshot": {"preparer": {"name": "SATISH PANDIAN S"}, "reviewers": [{"name": "43278"}, {"name": "36937"}, {"name": "36614"}]}, "noteDate": "2026-05-19"}

titleEn: Recommendation for Sponsoring IOB Branded Tickets at Sri Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple, Palani

{"deptName": "PLAN", "details": "

Introduction

Our Palani Branch has identified a strategic opportunity to enhance the visibility and brand recall of our Bank through sponsorship of IOB-branded thermal tickets used at Arulmigu Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple, Palani.

Observations

Palani Branch presently maintains a substantial share of the Temple’s deposit portfolio. The Branch has observed exceptionally high devotee footfall at the Temple throughout the year. Even during lean periods, the Temple attracts approximately 2–3 lakh devotees per month, while during festival and peak seasons, such volumes are witnessed on a daily basis. Devotees visiting the Temple originate from various parts of the country, thereby providing a unique and large-scale branding opportunity for the Bank. In this connection, the Branch proposes to sponsor IOB-branded thermal ticket rolls used for Temple entry/access. Such branding is expected to significantly improve the visibility of the Bank among the general public and reinforce our association with one of the most prominent pilgrimage centres in the country. The Branch has obtained a quotation from Mukil Printers, the regular supplier of thermal ticket rolls to the Temple authorities, for the following: 300 Nos. of 2-inch printed thermal rolls (15 metres) 500 Nos. of 3-inch printed thermal rolls (40 metres) The total cost for the above requirement is Rs.68,250/-. Introduction

Our Palani Branch (0376) has identified a strategic opportunity to enhance the visibility and brand recall of our Bank through sponsorship of IOB-branded thermal tickets used at Arulmigu Dhandayuthapani Swamy Temple, Palani.

Observations

Palani Branch presently maintains a substantial share of the Temple’s deposit portfolio. The Branch has observed exceptionally high devotee footfall at the Temple throughout the year. Even during lean periods, the Temple attracts approximately 2–3 lakh devotees per month, while during festival and peak seasons, such volumes are witnessed on a daily basis. Devotees visiting the Temple originate from various parts of the country, thereby providing a unique and large-scale branding opportunity for the Bank. In this connection, the Branch proposes to sponsor IOB-branded thermal ticket rolls used for Temple entry/access. Such branding is expected to significantly improve the visibility of the Bank among the general public and reinforce our association with one of the most prominent pilgrimage centres in the country. The Branch has obtained a quotation from Mukil Printers, the regular supplier of thermal ticket rolls to the Temple authorities, for the following: 300 Nos. of 2-inch printed thermal rolls (15 metres) 500 Nos. of 3-inch printed thermal rolls (40 metres) The total cost for the above requirement is Rs.68,250/-. The proposed quantity is estimated to cater to approximately 5,00,000 tickets and is expected to last for nearly two months.

Recommendations

Considering: the immense daily public exposure, the nationwide reach among devotees, the existing deposit relationship of over Rs.30 Crore maintained by the Temple with our Bank, and the strong potential for further business development, we may recommend sponsorship of the IOB-branded thermal tickets at a total cost of Rs.68,250/- to Public Relations Department, Central Office.", "signatorySnapshot": {"preparer": {"name": "SATISH PANDIAN"}, "reviewers": [{"name": "43278"}, {"name": "36937"}, {"name": "36614"}]}, "noteDate": "2026-05-19"}
